2025-04-04.thesis: China Announcement 18 bans 7 rare-earth exports April 4
2025-04-04.exit_case: Announcement 18 is now public; initial supply-shock news out and potentially priced into day-one moves.
2025-04-04.assessment: Day 1 of a structural rare-earth supply shock. China's Announcement 18 imposes export controls on 7 medium/heavy rare earths — samarium, gadolinium, terbium, dysprosium, lutetium, scandium, yttrium — materials critical for defense magnets, EV motors, and wind turbines. The announcement is the START of the restriction regime, not its resolution; controls are now actively in force and will persist until explicitly reversed. HK Law's article names MP Materials as 'the lone scaled US producer,' making it the cleanest pure-play. The retail audience is overwhelmed by Liberation Day tariff chaos — this specific rare-earth ban is still under-the-radar for most investors. Concrete catalysts still ahead: licensing implementation details, US DoD/DoE emergency stockpiling orders, congressional response, allied-nation supply diversification deals, and potential Pentagon off-take agreements with MP. REMX captures diversified non-Chinese rare earth producers globally (including Lynas). UUUU is a secondary play via its rare earth processing line at its White Mesa Mill. Hold all three; the driving condition — Chinese export controls actively restricting supply of 7 strategic materials — has not reversed.
2025-04-04.exit_advice: Exit only if China formally lifts or materially relaxes Announcement 18 export controls, or a US-China deal explicitly restores rare-earth supply flows to pre-April 4 levels.

This week's entire news feed — Indian equity markets (Sensex/Nifty +447-448 pts on US CPI cooling), renewable-energy stock screeners (Quanta Services, WEC Energy, Clearway), nuclear stock screeners (Oklo, NuScale, Centrus, BWX, Nano Nuclear), Canadian equity wraps (TSX recovering on inflation data), India AI stock bubble (RRP Semiconductor 55,000% surge), Tata iPhone ambitions, hedge funds rotating from energy to tech/AI, Bursa Malaysia profit-taking — is entirely orthogonal to China's rare-earth export-control thesis. Zero fresh coverage of Announcement 18, the seven controlled elements (samarium, gadolinium, terbium, dysprosium, lutetium, scandium, yttrium), MP Materials, REMX, or UUUU. No silence-decay reset applies. The devil's-advocate exit case is its sharpest post-Oct-17: that reset was anchored to two concrete hooks — (1) new Chinese rare-earth curbs confirmed Oct. 6–10 by Japan Times, CNBC TV18, and Moneycontrol, and (2) Trump's explicitly threatened 100% additional tariff with a specific Nov. 1 date. The Nov. 1 date has now passed for eight consecutive weeks without confirmed implementation or formal cancellation, removing the only near-term scheduled anchor. What remains ahead are entirely unscheduled, undated pending catalysts — MOFCOM licensing and implementation guidance for the seven elements, congressional rare-earth legislation still in committee, further DoE/DoD procurement covering the broader element basket (directly benefiting REMX and UUUU), and allied-nation diversification deals — none with a concrete announced date, meaning the 'concrete scheduled catalyst' bar is not currently met. Nevertheless, per the structural-driver rule, this thesis exits only on genuine reversal of the underlying supply-restriction regime (controls lifted, MOFCOM suspension published), not on silence or a tariff date passing without follow-through. China's Announcement 18 export-control regime remains fully in force and unreversed; the Oct. 6–10 new curbs are confirmed real policy; and the DoD $400M MP Materials preferred-stock deal (July 11) remains an open structural commitment anchoring US supply-chain urgency. MP remains the cleanest pure-play (DoD as largest shareholder) with elevated post-surge crowding risk; REMX and UUUU remain relatively less crowded structural expressions of the same driver and are fully warranted. All three vehicles remain structurally valid; no compositional change warranted. At conviction 1 (absolute floor), the position is outcompeted and culled from the active shortlist. A single concrete fresh milestone — MOFCOM implementation detail, a congressional bill advancing, an allied procurement announcement, or a DoD/DoE follow-on order — would reset conviction upward and re-qualify the basket.
2025-12-19.exit_advice: Hold; do not exit until MOFCOM formally suspends Announcement 18 controls — silence and lapsed tariff dates are not reversals of the structural supply-restriction regime.
